message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Close frankentorch-awo (label_smoothing, weight_norm, spectral_norm,
Subset, random_split — 12 new tests, 2714 passing) and
frankentorch-b6n (ft-data loading path audit); open
frankentorch-u0p (ft-serialize round-trip/format audit, in_progress).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankentorch-alm","title":"Implement functional API: normalize, dropout, embedding, linear","description":"Add session-level functional ops matching torch.nn.functional: normalize (Lp normalization along dim), dropout (random zeroing), embedding (lookup table), linear (y=xA^T+b). These are commonly used in custom model forward() methods outside of nn.Module.","notes":"Verified that ft-api already implements the requested session-level functional ops: functional_normalize, functional_dropout, functional_embedding, and functional_linear. Confirmed with targeted rch-offloaded tests: cargo test -p ft-api functional_ -- --nocapture (10 passed).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T19:20:50.949752813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T19:23:33.532023017Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T19:23:33.531077685Z","close_reason":"Implemented functional_normalize (Lp/dim), functional_dropout (inverted scaling), functional_embedding (lookup with 2D indices), functional_linear (matmul+bias). 10 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-ap0","title":"Align core FrankenTorch spec with actual workspace and local oracle assumptions","description":"The main FrankenTorch spec still uses the older simplified architecture line and treats legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch as always present. Update only the core repo-map/oracle wording so the normative spec matches the current checkout and crate layout.","notes":"Updated the top-level FrankenTorch spec so the architecture blueprint and crate-family list match the current workspace, and the PyTorch oracle path is documented as a typical optional local mirror instead of an always-present checkout.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:59:41.440546462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:00:05.575773878Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:00:05.575137443Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-aqm","title":"Add compile-time exhaustiveness guard for DType match arms","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found 26 unreachable!() calls. Many exist because match arms on DType handle a subset of variants and use unreachable!() for the rest after an early validation check. This pattern is fragile: if a new DType variant is added, the early check might be updated but the match arm might not, causing a panic.

## Problem
The DType enum has 9 variants (F64, F32, F16, BF16, I64, I32, Bool, Complex64, Complex128). Functions that only handle a subset (e.g., floating-point types) use an early if-guard then unreachable!() in the catch-all. A better pattern is to use a helper that converts the subset to a sub-enum, making the match exhaustive at compile time.

## Plan
1. Create typed helper enums or use existing type-level dispatch patterns:
   - FloatingDType { F64, F32, F16, BF16 } with TryFrom<DType>
   - NumericDType { F64, F32, I64, I32 } with TryFrom<DType>
2. Convert the most dangerous unreachable!() sites (those in hot paths like dispatch and backward) to use these sub-enums
3. The match becomes exhaustive and the compiler catches missing arms on enum changes
4. Less critical sites (test helpers, edge cases) can keep unreachable!() with messages

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least the 14 unreachable!() sites in ft-dispatch are converted to exhaustive matches
- New DType variants cause compile errors (not runtime panics) in dispatch code
- All existing tests pass

## Dependencies
- Depends on: frankentorch-dwy (DenseTensor::storage panic fix) -- both touch DType handling
- Should be done AFTER the storage() refactor to avoid merge conflicts

## Reasoning
This is the highest-leverage defense-in-depth change. It converts 14 potential runtime panics into compile-time errors, which aligns with the DAC correctness doctrine and Rust's zero-cost abstraction philosophy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:23:32.701520867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T23:54:37.770060012Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T23:54:37.769695201Z","close_reason":"Replaced all 14 ft-dispatch DType/promotion unreachable!() sites with typed non-complex storage classification, added regression tests for complex rejection and BF16 fallback, and verified ft-dispatch tests plus workspace check/clippy via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-aqm","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-dwy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:24:13.645570811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankentorch-awo","title":"Add cross_entropy label_smoothing, weight_norm, spectral_norm, random_split, Subset","description":"Implemented: cross_entropy_loss_with_smoothing (label smoothing parameter), weight_norm_decompose/reconstruct, spectral_norm, Subset (Arc-based), random_split. 12 new tests, all 2714 workspace tests passing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:28:22.787747419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:28:27.120996753Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:28:27.120748252Z","close_reason":"Implemented and tested. 12 new tests, all 2714 workspace tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-b6n","title":"Audit ft-data loading path for latent correctness bugs","description":"## Context
The explicit parity backlog is empty, but we still need fresh-eyes subsystem audits to find latent correctness bugs hidden behind current green status. Start with ft-data and trace its batching/shuffle/drop_last behavior through tests and adjacent crate usage.

## Goal
Identify and fix any obvious correctness bug in the ft-data loading path, then verify with focused tests and workspace gates.

## Acceptance
- trace ft-data end-to-end
- fix at least one high-confidence correctness bug if found
- add/adjust tests proving the fix
- verify with rch cargo check/clippy/tests","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:10:45.588213207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:20:40.140473566Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:20:40.140223743Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Restore those gates without regressing current parity work.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-01T20:42:25.183678811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T03:26:33.195183827Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T03:26:33.194775333Z","close_reason":"Restored fmt/clippy workspace gate drift in ft-data and ft-nn; verified fmt locally and clippy via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-tk5","title":"Enrich Module register_parameter/register_buffer errors with module type context","description":"## Context
The stub/placeholder audit (2026-04-02) found that Module::register_parameter() and Module::register_buffer() default implementations (ft-nn/src/lib.rs) return `Err(ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported { operation: "register_parameter" })`.

The error tells you WHAT operation failed but not WHICH module rejected it. When debugging a deep Sequential model with 50+ layers, "register_parameter unsupported" gives no clue about which layer is the problem.

## Current error type
```rust
pub enum ModuleRegistrationError {
    InvalidName { kind: &'static str },
    NameConflict { name: String },
    Unsupported { operation: &'static str },
}
```

## Design decision: How to get the module type name in a trait default

The trait default method signature is `fn register_parameter(&mut self, ...)`. We CANNOT call `std::any::type_name::<Self>()` because the default method has `Self: ?Sized` (it's on `dyn Module`).

**Solution**: Add a new required method to the Module trait:
```rust
fn module_type_name(&self) -> &'static str;
```
This is cheap (one &'static str per impl), gives correct type info even through dyn dispatch, and follows the same pattern PyTorch uses with `_get_name()`.

Each module implementation returns its name: "Linear", "Conv2d", "Sequential", etc.

The default for register_parameter/register_buffer then becomes:
```rust
fn register_parameter(...) -> Result<(), ModuleRegistrationError> {
    Err(ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported {
        operation: "register_parameter",
        module_type: self.module_type_name(),
    })
}
```

## Plan
1. Add `module_type_name(&self) -> &'static str` to the Module trait (no default — every impl must provide it)
2. Add `module_type: &'static str` field to `ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported`
3. Update the Display impl to print "{module_type} does not support {operation}"
4. Update default register_parameter/register_buffer to include module_type
5. Add `module_type_name()` to ALL existing Module implementations (~40+ structs):
   - Linear -> "Linear", Conv1d -> "Conv1d", Sequential -> "Sequential", etc.
6. Update any tests that construct or match ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported

## Testing requirements

### Unit tests (in ft-nn #[cfg(test)])

#### test_unsupported_register_includes_module_type
- Call register_parameter on a module that doesn't override it (e.g., ReLU)
- Assert the error message contains "ReLU"
- Assert the error message contains "register_parameter"

#### test_unsupported_register_buffer_includes_module_type
- Same for register_buffer on a leaf module

#### test_all_modules_have_type_name
- For each major module type (Linear, Conv1d, Conv2d, LSTM, Sequential, etc.):
  - Construct an instance
  - Assert module_type_name() returns a non-empty string
  - Assert module_type_name() does NOT contain "Module" alone (should be specific)

#### test_module_type_name_matches_struct_name
- For a selection of modules, verify that module_type_name() matches the Rust struct name
- E.g., Linear::module_type_name() == "Linear"

#### test_sequential_register_parameter_succeeds
- Verify that Sequential (which DOES support register_parameter) still works correctly
- Construct a Sequential, register a parameter, verify it appears in named_parameter_slots_own()

### E2E validation
- Run `rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn` — all existing + new tests pass
- Run `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ft-nn --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean

## Acceptance Criteria
- Error messages include the module type name (e.g., "ReLU does not support register_parameter")
- ALL Module implementations have module_type_name()
- 5+ new tests covering the enriched error paths
- Existing tests still pass
- Clippy clean

## Risk
Medium — this adds a required trait method to ~40 implementations. Use grep to find all `impl Module for` blocks. Each just needs one line added. Can be parallelized with subagents.

## Dependencies
None — isolated change within ft-nn.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T23:23:05.244308597Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T00:26:31.066854625Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T00:26:31.066605804Z","close_reason":"ModuleRegistrationError::Unsupported now carries module_type context, default Module registration methods report std::any::type_name::<Self>(), and ft-nn test plus workspace check/clippy passed via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
